_comment: Declarative UI definition for the OrcaSlicer Profile Generator GUI. The GUI (gui.py) renders itself entirely from this file - it contains NO hard-coded printers, filaments, or options. To expose a new printer, filament, or option, add an entry here; no Python changes are needed. Current values are persisted separately in config.json; the 'default' fields below are only used on first run when no saved value exists.

app.title: OrcaSlicer Profile Generator
app.subtitle: Algorithmic process / machine / filament profiles for Bambu Lab printers

groups.title: Printers
groups.help: Enable the printer(s) you own. Profiles are generated for every enabled printer across all four nozzle sizes (0.2 / 0.4 / 0.6 / 0.8 mm).

groups.title: Optional Filaments
groups.help: Core filaments (PLA, PETG, ABS) are always generated. Toggle the specialty filaments you use.

options.label: PLA Silk
options.help: Shimmer PLA - own process profile (low accel)

options.label: PLA Wood
options.help: Wood-fill PLA - uses PLA process, low volumetric speed

options.label: PLA-CF
options.help: Carbon fiber PLA - uses PLA process, needs hardened nozzle

options.label: BVOH
options.help: Dissolvable support - better than PVA, works with PETG, requires AMS

groups.title: Output & Options
groups.help: Where to write the generated profiles and how to run.

fields.label: Write profiles to
fields.help: 'OrcaSlicer config' writes into your live OrcaSlicer user profiles (a timestamped backup is always taken first). 'Custom folder' writes a standalone copy and never touches OrcaSlicer.

fields.label: Custom output folder
fields.help: Folder to write the process / machine / filament subfolders into.

fields.label: Clean existing generated profiles first
fields.help: Delete all previously generated 'B' profiles before writing. A backup is always taken first. Use this for a full reset.

fields.label: Dry run (preview only - write nothing)
fields.help: Run the full generation and report what would happen without writing or deleting any files.
